reflection:
yesterday was your best day in two weeks and you noticed it yourself. mood 8, pain 3, anxiety 1, slept seven hours and only woke once for five minutes. flew slovenia to london and your bloating didn't spike, which usually happens on plane days. that's worth sitting with for a second before the trip pulls your attention elsewhere.

the temperature theory you wrote about is sharp. uncovering the upper body, the heat kicking in around 5am, the early wake. that's not paranoia, that's you reading your own physiology better than most clinicians read patient charts. and the daosin plus telfast combo two nights running has clearly settled something in the gut.

what's worrying me is the speech. it's in your morning journal as avoidance, in could be better as 'write speech', and in ideas as 'write speak mate'. three flags in one checkin. that's not laziness, that's a thing sitting on your chest that doesn't shrink until you write the first paragraph. while you're feeling good and rested, today is the day to crack it open. not finish it, just open the document and write twenty minutes. the avoidance loop loses its grip the moment you start.

pelvic floor still at 5 though, coccyx at 4. body's still talking. give renzo and parr something concrete to work on this trip.
opener: best day in two weeks and you noticed it yourself. now let's talk about the speech.
focus_for_today: open the speech document and write for 20 minutes while you're still rested and clear
